title: hierarchy -chparam cannot decode string parameter value
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
example.v
```verilog
module example #(
    // TEST can be "PASS" or "INV"
    parameter TEST = "PASS"
)(
    input a,
    output b
);

always @(*)
    if(TEST=="PASS")
        b = a;
    else
        b = ~a;

endmodule
```
```tcl
read -formal example.v

hierarchy -top example -chparam TEST "PASS"
prep -top example
```
I am yosys with cmd `yosys design.ys`
## Expected behavior
Parameter TEST is expected assign with value "PASS"

## Actual behavior
But I got
```bash

 Yosys 0.9+4052 (git sha1 d061b0e4, clang 6.0.0-1ubuntu2 -fPIC -Os)


-- Executing script file `design.ys' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: example.v
Parsing formal SystemVerilog input from `example.v' to AST representation.
Storing AST representation for module `$abstract\example'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

2. Executing HIERARCHY pass (managing design hierarchy).
ERROR: Can't decode value '"PASS"'!
```
I check the help of cmd **hierarchy**, it says
```text
    -chparam name value 
       elaborate the top module using this parameter value. Modules on which
       this parameter does not exist may cause a warning message to be output.
       This option can be specified multiple times to override multiple
       parameters. String values must be passed in double quotes (").
```
Then I try cmd `chparam -set TEST "PASS"`, it works fine
Any idea?
code_blocks:
Yosys 0.9+4052 (git sha1 d061b0e4, clang 6.0.0-1ubuntu2 -fPIC -Os)


-- Executing script file `design.ys' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: example.v
Parsing formal SystemVerilog input from `example.v' to AST representation.
Storing AST representation for module `$abstract\example'.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.

2. Executing HIERARCHY pass (managing design hierarchy).
ERROR: Can't decode value '"PASS"'!

-chparam name value 
       elaborate the top module using this parameter value. Modules on which
       this parameter does not exist may cause a warning message to be output.
       This option can be specified multiple times to override multiple
       parameters. String values must be passed in double quotes (").
